content.claim: Rural school bus commutes in general rural education literature exceed 30 minutes on average and are longer than urban counterparts, with longer commutes associated with skipped breakfast, lower grades, poorer fitness, fewer social activities, and worse study habits, disproportionately burdening lower-income households
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Finding is drawn from non-Hungarian rural education literature and applied analogically to Hungarian context
No Hungary-specific quantitative study of commute effects on rural pupils found in this search
General findings may not reflect specific geography, infrastructure, or demographic composition of Hungarian villages
Income-based burden is inferred from household economics rather than directly measured for Hungarian rural students
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
